paragraphs.text: Signet is the world's largest diamond retailer, Signet purchases natural diamonds and laboratory-grown diamonds from a global supply chain network. In turn, we sell both laboratory-grown and natural diamonds to our customers in the US, Canada, and the UK.

section_path: Signet Jewelers' Response to the EC Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act

paragraphs.text: As a founding member and five-time certified member of the Responsible Jewellery Council (RJC), Signet leaders engage with regulators, NGOs, and other industry bodies to benefit the overall reputation of the jewelry industry. The RJC is the standard-setting body of the jewelry industry created to add integrity to all facets of the jewelry industry. RCJ standards cover both natural and laboratory-grown diamonds.

section_path: Signet Jewelers' Response to the EC Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act

evidence_quotes.quote: unambiguously disclose laboratory-grown diamonds from natural diamonds in the online marketplace to ensure better transparency and clarity for consumers.

paragraphs.text: Signet welcomes the European Commission's (Commission) commitment to modernize European consumer protection legislation applicable to online markets and ensure a level playing field for online traders through the forthcoming Digital Fairness Act (DFA). Signet believes clear guidance and standards relating to the disclosure of laboratory-grown diamonds help prevent misleading claims and customer confusion which in turn promotes consumer confidence in the retail market. Signet suggests requirements that unambiguously disclose laboratory-grown diamonds from natural diamonds in the online marketplace to ensure better transparency and clarity for consumers.

section_path: Signet Jewelers' Response to the EC Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act

evidence_quotes.quote: some existing marketing practices, especially online and on social media, do not make this distinction clear, which is misleading for consumers and can lead to consumer confusion, and potentially to consumer harm.

paragraphs.text: The retail marketplace has long-offered lower-cost simulants for natural diamonds such as cubic zirconia and moissanite. As technology has advanced, laboratory-grown diamonds have become more prevalent in the marketplace as they can be mass produced at increasingly lower prices. Natural diamonds (i.e. those created by natural geological processes within the Earth's mantle which are then subsequently recovered) and laboratory-grown diamonds (i.e. those that are man-made or laboratory-grown) are different products, with distinct origins and market values. Despite this, some existing marketing practices, especially online and on social media, do not make this distinction clear, which is misleading for consumers and can lead to consumer confusion, and potentially to consumer harm. Regarding clear disclosure to consumers, Signet adheres to the RJC Laboratory Grown Material Standard LGMS 27: Product Disclosure regarding the acceptable terms used to disclose laboratory-grown diamonds in the public marketplace. Signet advises using this industry-developed standard as a foundation for EU standards.

section_path: Signet Jewelers' Response to the EC Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act

section_path: Signet Jewelers' Response to the EC Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act

paragraphs.text: Natural and laboratory-grown diamonds appear the same to the naked eye. Signet requires all suppliers to comply with the Signet Responsible Sourcing Program (SRSP), which includes proper disclosure, segregation, and labeling of natural and laboratory-grown diamonds. In addition, Signet is a participant in the ASSURE Project, a testing regimen for Diamond Verification Instruments. A Diamond Verification Instrument is a device used to separate and/or identify natural diamonds from laboratory-grown diamonds. Signet and other retailers use this specialized equipment to ensure laboratory-grown diamonds are properly segmented and not cross-contaminated with natural diamonds to ensure integrity in the supply chain and the trust of our customers.

section_path: Signet Jewelers' Response to the EC Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act

evidence_quotes.quote: properly disclose if it is a laboratory-grown diamond at the point of sale and in marketing materials

paragraphs.text: Consumers do not have access to specialized equipment and therefore it is imperative that retailers properly disclose if it is a laboratory-grown diamond at the point of sale and in marketing materials, particularly in online retail and advertising campaigns.

section_path: Signet Jewelers' Response to the EC Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act

evidence_quotes.quote: This can lead to consumers paying highly inflated prices for a product (laboratory-grown diamonds) relative to the inherent value.

paragraphs.text: Clear mandatory standards that require unambiguous disclosure of laboratory-grown diamonds will better protect EU consumers online by reducing the scope for unfair and misleading business practices and disinformation. Lack of disclosure on laboratory-grown diamonds in online markets and social media, could potentially confuse consumers. This can lead to consumers paying highly inflated prices for a product (laboratory-grown diamonds) relative to the inherent value.

section_path: Signet Jewelers' Response to the EC Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act

paragraphs.text: Signet has observed overly generalized or unqualified sustainability claims. Consumers may perceive the term "sustainable," or similar terms, differently. Signet supports sustainability standards that require manufacturers and retailers to provide clear, detailed, and substantiated claims. Both natural diamond and laboratory-grown diamond production have variations in production processes and methodology. Sustainability claims encompass various dimensions, including environmental, social, and traceability aspects. By providing specific information, these claims enable consumers to evaluate the comprehensive environmental and social impacts of both the production process and the finished jewelry product.

section_path: Signet Jewelers' Response to the EC Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act

section_path: Signet Jewelers' Response to the EC Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act

evidence_quotes.quote: The DFA has the key objective of preventing misleading marketing by influencers and in social media campaigns.

paragraphs.text: The DFA has the key objective of preventing misleading marketing by influencers and in social media campaigns. To help protect consumers and support the health and integrity of the diamond industry while addressing unfair marketing practices, Signet recommends that the Commission:

Introduce EU-wide disclosure requirements for laboratory-grown diamonds. This could be achieved by bringing ISO standard 18323 into EU binding law to ensure proper consumer information and disclosure of laboratory-grown products.

evidence_quotes.quote: their right to request online platforms remove or correct online information that is clearly misleading or false

paragraphs.text: Ensure companies can quickly and efficiently exercise their right to request online platforms remove or correct online information that is clearly misleading or false, or otherwise contrary to EU-wide mandatory standards.
Introduce an impactful, stringent regime for online retailers and social media influencers that repeatedly provide misleading or false advertising content.

evidence_quotes.quote: The Commission should introduce clear, EU-wide rules that require disclosure of natural products and laboratory-grown products.

paragraphs.text: The Commission should introduce clear, EU-wide rules that require disclosure of natural products and laboratory-grown products. "Natural" is an important marker of the origin and sourcing of a product and should be protected from misleading claims that deceive consumers.

We appreciate the opportunity to input into this consultation ahead of what could be a significant step forward in the protection of EU consumers online and look forward to further engagement with the Commission.
p 330.668.5000 | 375 Ghent Rd Akron, OH 44333
